providers.title: Model providers
providers.description: Manage built-in provider connections, or add OpenAI-compatible custom providers.
providers.reasoningOn: Reasoning output is returned and shown for this provider.
providers.reasoningOff: Reasoning content is hidden; for MiniMax it is automatically separated and cleaned to keep <think> out of the prose.
providers.balanceCustomNotice: Custom OpenAI-compatible providers don't support balance lookup yet.

providerDialog.apiKeyOptionalNotice: API Key can be empty; once you fill the API URL we can fetch the model list and pick a default for you.
providerDialog.apiKeyConfiguredPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved API Key
providerDialog.baseUrlHintCreating: Enter an OpenAI-compatible API URL, usually ending with /v1; local Ollama is typically http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1.
providerDialog.baseUrlHintEditing: Leave empty to use the default URL; local Ollama is typically http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1.
providerDialog.primaryModelHintCreate: After fetching the list, the first model is filled automatically; if the endpoint returns no list, you can type one manually.
providerDialog.primaryModelHintCustomEmpty: Use the “Refresh models” button on the provider card to fetch the list, or type the default model manually.
providerDialog.primaryModelHintBuiltin: If the model you need is missing from the list, type it manually.
providerDialog.modelManualPlaceholder: Or type the model name manually
providerDialog.imageModelHint: When set, this provider can be used for character portrait generation; otherwise it stays text-only.
providerDialog.imageModelFootnote: Image generation calls this provider's OpenAI-compatible image API.
providerDialog.requestLimitConcurrencyHint: 0 means unlimited. Requests above the cap for the same provider and model are queued.
providerDialog.requestLimitIntervalHint: 0 means unlimited. Controls how quickly requests can fire for the same provider and model.

navigation.ragDescription: Configure embedding model, index scope and retrieval parameters inside the knowledge base module.
navigation.modelRouteDescription: Pick a default provider and model for each writing task.
navigation.modelRouteSummary: Manage the models used for outlining, chapter breakdown, drafting, review and other tasks in one place.

styleEngine.title: Style engine runtime
styleEngine.description: Controls how long style extraction waits for the model. Long source texts can use a higher value; short texts benefit from a shorter timeout so anomalies surface fast.
styleEngine.rangeHint: Allowed range: {{min}}–{{max}} minutes. Default {{defaultValue}} minutes. New and retried style extraction jobs use this timeout once saved.
styleEngine.invalidRange: Enter an integer between {{min}} and {{max}} minutes.
styleEngine.savedFailed: Failed to save style engine runtime.

autoDirectorApproval.title: Approval preferences
autoDirectorApproval.description: When the auto-director runs over a range, it starts from these default authorizations. Approval points without authorization still pause for your confirmation.

autoDirectorChannel.title: Director follow-up channels
autoDirectorChannel.description: Configure DingTalk and WeCom webhook, callback token, operator mapping and event subscriptions in one place. Without complete callback wiring, messages quietly downgrade to in-app links only.
autoDirectorChannel.siteBaseUrlHint: Used in “Open follow-up center / View details” links in DingTalk/WeCom messages. Falls back to the server's site URL when empty.
autoDirectorChannel.callbackTokenPlaceholder: Optional; without it, messages only deep-link in-app
autoDirectorChannel.savedFailed: Failed to save director follow-up channels.

approvalRequired.label: Awaiting auto-continue
approvalRequired.description: Notify when the auto-director pauses at a continue or confirmation step.

autoApproved.label: AI auto-approved
autoApproved.description: Notify when the system passes a checkpoint via your approval preferences and keeps running.

exception.label: Runtime error
exception.description: Notify when the auto-director hits an error, fails, or enters an exception state.

recovered.label: Recovered
recovered.description: Notify when a previously failed auto-director task resumes successfully.

completed.label: Completed
completed.description: Notify when an auto-director task finishes the current stage or full pipeline.

progressChanged.label: Progress change
progressChanged.description: Notify when the auto-director crosses a stage boundary or hits a key progress milestone.

modelRoutes.pageDescription: Pick the right model for each creative task and verify the JSON output stays stable.
modelRoutes.detectionNotice: Detection covers plain dialog and structured output; form edits only count once saved.
modelRoutes.bulkDescription: Pick a model set once, then push it to many tasks; a single save makes new generation use the new routes.
modelRoutes.bulkStatus: {{dirty}} task(s) pending save; {{failed}} task(s) failing checks; {{empty}} empty route(s).
modelRoutes.bulkApplied: Filled the model into {{count}} task(s); save to make it effective.
modelRoutes.savedRoute: Saved. This task will use the new route.
modelRoutes.savedRoutes: Saved. {{count}} task(s) will use the new routes.
modelRoutes.fallbackDescription: When the main model chats fine but produces unstable JSON, you can enable a shared fallback model for every structured task.
modelRoutes.fallbackEnableHint: We only switch to the fallback after every structured strategy on the main model fails.
modelRoutes.modelEmptyText: This provider has no models available
modelRoutes.modelManualPlaceholder: Or type the model name manually
modelRoutes.maxTokensPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the system default
modelRoutes.dirtyHint: Form changes take effect after saving.
modelRoutes.unsavedDiff: These results reflect the live route; the system re-tests after you save.

planner.title: Outline planner
planner.description: Reads the creative goal first, then plans how the current stretch should move forward.

writer.title: Main writer
writer.description: Generates chapter prose and writes the full chapter body.

review.title: General review
review.description: Checks plot, pacing and voice, surfacing quality issues in the draft.

light_review.title: Quick review
light_review.description: Quickly decides whether a chapter can move on; a light quality check after writing.

critical_review.title: Strict review
critical_review.description: Handles quality checks that touch novel-wide continuity; suited for high-risk reviews and re-checks.

repair.title: Chapter repair
repair.description: Repairs the manuscript per review notes so the chapter can keep moving forward.

replan.title: Window replan
replan.description: Reorganises goals and bridges of the affected chapters when local repair cannot converge.

state_resolution.title: State resolution
state_resolution.description: Judges whether chapter state proposals are trustworthy, reducing manual confirmation for the auto-director.

summary.title: Plot summary
summary.description: Distils long chapters into recaps, summaries and key changes.

fact_extraction.title: Lore extraction
fact_extraction.description: Organises settings, timelines and key facts to reduce contradictions over time.

chat.title: Co-writer chat
chat.description: Handles everyday chat and shapes the output into something writing can use directly.
